summary: google-caja-bower exfiltrates installer project files to Discord webhook via preinstall
details: The package declares scripts.preinstall = 'node index.js', so index.js runs automatically on npm install. The script recursively walks process.cwd() (up to 500 files), reads non-binary file contents under an 8 MB cap via fs.readdirSync/fs.readFileSync, and POSTs each file — plus a directory tree, os.hostname(), os.platform(), and USER/USERNAME — to a hardcoded Discord webhook at discord.com/api/webhooks/1525544748444680392/... using https.request multipart uploads. Files bulk-uploaded include any.env, config, and credential files sitting in the developer's project tree. The package name and inflated version (google-caja-bower@999.99.20) impersonate the deprecated google-caja/bower ecosystem in a dependency-confusion shape intended to override an internal package during resolution, and the exfil message body labels itself 'Dependency Confusion Executed'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
